name: British East India Company (1801–1858)
description: Thirteen alternating red and white horizontal stripes, with the British Union Flag in the upper hoist canton.
history: The flag of the British East India Company. Earlier versions (from the 17th century) used the cross of St George in the canton; after the 1801 Acts of Union the canton became the new Union Flag (with the saltire of St Patrick added). The flag is often cited as a possible inspiration for the early flags of the United States. The Company was dissolved in 1874 but lost its administrative role following the Indian Rebellion of 1857.
